fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Virginia Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Virginia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity virginia, how to transfer audiologist license to virginia, audiologist compact virginia, virginia audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Virginia.

steps.title: Meet Educational Requirements
steps.description: You must have a doctoral degree in audiology from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Experience
steps.description: Complete supervised professional experience as defined in the Virginia Administrative Code.

steps.title: Pass the Examination and Apply for Licensure
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Audiology Examination administered by Praxis and submit your application with the required fee and documentation to the Virginia Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Virginia audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Virginia have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Virginia participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Virginia without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Virginia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Virginia
hero.dek: Virginia Audiologist license requires a doctoral degree in audiology, passing the Audiology Examination, and completion of a supervised experience.
